title: Neuropathy follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings
source.population: Adults with neuropathic pain managed in non-specialist settings.
source.setting: Non-specialist assessment, treatment review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult neuropathic-pain review and treatment monitoring; partial for a generic neuropathy workflow and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

assessment.text: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
source.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
source.setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

assessment.text: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.
